behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "grammatically coherent" and "preserves the meaning". Slot B (decisive boundary): model-output quality from a real neural translation model. Slot C (miss if lowered one level): at Integration with stubbed outputs, semantic drift/literal-but-wrong translations still pass. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because the behavior is a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps. Final label: Agentic due to model-output boundary (Slot B) and the missed semantic-quality failure at Integration (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model on a multilingual evaluation set and assert meaning-preservation/coherence quality thresholds (e.g., human-validated set or robust semantic scoring), failing on materially incorrect but fluent outputs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires real model judgments; a lower-level stub cannot expose semantic-quality failures.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "API returns a 503" and service "falls back ... without returning an error". Slot B (decisive boundary): module handoff/orchestration between API client, fallback translator, and response layer. Slot C (miss if lowered one level): Unit tests of fallback logic can miss wiring bugs where 503 is not mapped to fallback path in service orchestration. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; a deterministic stubbed 503 is sufficient. (b) correctness here does not depend on model output quality. (c) no multi-step agentic chain. (d) no LLM-driven stage handoff. Rejection-gate: System rejected because real network behavior is not required by this behavior text. Final label: Integration due to handoff boundary (Slot B) and missed service-wiring failure at Unit (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real service modules with a stub API client that returns HTTP 503 and a real/local fallback translator path; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no surfaced error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Description verifies cross-module fallback behavior; this cannot be proven by an isolated function test.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "pre-stored translation" in "SQLite database". Slot B (decisive boundary): service/repository/database handoff with local DB I/O. Slot C (miss if lowered one level): Unit tests with in-memory maps can miss SQL/query/schema mismatches that return wrong rows or no rows. Integration-brake: (a) no external OS/network/subprocess beyond local DB module wiring. (b) no AI/ML output dependence. (c) no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System rejected because local SQLite interaction is covered by Integration per level definition. Final label: Integration due to DB handoff boundary (Slot B) and schema/query misses at Unit (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Use a real SQLite test DB seeded with known phrase pairs, call fallback translator through service/repo layers, and assert exact returned translations for existing phrases.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real SQLite wiring to catch query/persistence issues; unit-only mocks are insufficient.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "computes edit distance ... and stores the metric". Slot B (decisive boundary): compute + persistence handoff (quality module to storage). Slot C (miss if lowered one level): Unit tests can validate distance math but miss persistence mapping/transaction bugs where metric is not saved or saved in wrong field. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required. (b) no dependence on real model output quality; fixed strings are sufficient. (c) no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior targets deterministic metric computation/storage, not model judgment quality. Final label: Integration due to compute-to-storage handoff (Slot B) and persistence miss at Unit (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Invoke quality-tracking path with deterministic primary/fallback strings through wired modules and real local persistence, then assert stored edit-distance value matches expected.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The requirement includes storage, so isolated algorithm tests are too low.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: service "returns the primary translation" and "logs the quality comparison in the background". Slot B (decisive boundary): asynchronous orchestration/handoff between request path and background logging path. Slot C (miss if lowered one level): Unit tests miss race/ordering issues where response is delayed by logging or logging job never enqueued. Integration-brake: (a) no real external OS/network required; controllable in-process async components suffice. (b) behavior does not assert model-output quality, only routing/prioritization and side-effect scheduling. (c) no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text does not evaluate translation correctness, only orchestration when both are available. Final label: Integration due to async handoff boundary (Slot B) and race/enqueue misses at Unit (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: With both translator dependencies available (stubbed deterministic outputs), run the full service flow and assert immediate response uses primary output while background quality-log record is eventually persisted/enqueued.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The async side effect and cross-module timing require integrated components.
